programme.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
programme.desc: In Samurai Town, a ruthless bank robber gets sprung from jail by a warlord whose adopted granddaughter has gone missing. Strapped into a leather suit that will self-destruct in five days, the bandit sets off on a journey to find the young woman.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Strange events plague a troubled teen when she participates in a scientific experiment to stop her nightmares.

programme.title: The Invoking
programme.desc: Samantha inherits a house from family she never knew and goes to the property with three friends to see it. Shortly after their arrival, Samantha experiences horrific visions as the demons from her past refuse to stay buried.

programme.title: Feral
programme.desc: A wild animal attacks six grad students on a weekend hike in the woods. One by one, they become infected with an unknown virus that turns them into bloodthirsty creatures.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A plane takes off during a snowstorm and soon reports both pilots are dead. As the passengers slowly die off, they wish they had never left the ground.

programme.title: Cabin Fever: Patient Zero
programme.desc: Vacationers think they've found the perfect place for a bachelor party, but a deadly, flesh-eating virus is waiting to infect them.

programme.title: Impetigore
programme.desc: A woman inherits a house in her ancestral village, but she is unaware that members of the community have been trying to locate and kill her to remove the curse that has plagued them for years.

programme.title: Infected
programme.desc: Seven strangers try to escape from Los Angeles after a strange virus that mutates humans is released.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: Z
programme.desc: A boy's imaginary friend terrorizes his family.

programme.title: The Stranger
programme.desc: A mysterious man's arrival in a small Canadian town plunges the community into a bloodbath.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Caveat
programme.desc: A desperate drifter suffering from partial memory loss agrees to look after his landlord's psychologically troubled niece in an isolated island mansion.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.title: The Vigil
programme.desc: Terror strikes when an Orthodox Jewish man agrees to watch over the dead body of a community member in a dilapidated Brooklyn home.

programme.title: Hellbender
programme.desc: A teen and her mother live simply in a home in the woods, spending their time making metal music. A chance encounter with a fellow teen causes her to uncover a connection between her family and witchcraft, which causes a rift with her mother.

programme.title: Stranded
programme.desc: Astronauts (Christian Slater, Brendan Fehr) come under attack by dangerous alien spores after a meteor collides with their moon base.

programme.title: Moloch
programme.desc: When she and her family are attacked by a random stranger one night, a Dutch woman sets out to find an explanation. The more she digs, the more she becomes convinced that she is being hunted by something ancient.

programme.title: The Twin
programme.desc: What begins as a time of healing in the quiet Scandinavian countryside soon takes an ominous turn when a woman begins to unravel the torturous truth about her son and confronts the malicious forces that are trying to take hold of him.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.desc: A teen and his best friend endure nonstop torment from bullies at school, but that soon changes when one discovers a bloodthirsty creature that resides in a country shed.

programme.desc: Strange events plague an ex-convict (Jesse Bradford) when he moves into the sinister apartment of his late mother.

programme.title: Jazz 625 at the BBC
programme.desc: Programme celebrating the archive of this legendary BBC jazz concert show from the mid 1960s, so titled because the newly launched BBC 2 was broadcasting on 625-UHF lines (the HD of the time) unlike the 405-VHF of BBC 1 and ITV. It features classic performances from legends such as Duke Ellington, Dave Brubeck and Dizzy Gillespie and The Julian 'Cannonball' Adderley Sextet, alongside rarely seen sessions from the likes of Wes Montgomery, Marian McPartland, Chris Barber's Jazz Band and Willie 'The Lion' Smith.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 November 1999 and featuring Geri Halliwell, Travis, Ricky Martin, A1, Will Smith, Stereophonics, Prince and Robbie Williams.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 November 1999 and featuring Robbie Williams, Whitney Houston, Glamma Kid, Blur, Cliff Richard, Texas and Wamdue Project.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, filmed in 1977 from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Frankie Vaughan, Mike and Bernie Winters, Maryetta Midgley, Zena Millar and members of the Players Theatre, London.

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Dusty Springfield's 1960s variety show, first broadcast on 15 August 1967, with the voices of Madeline Bell, Leslie Duncan and Maggie Stredder. Her special guests are Alf Garnett aka actor Warren Mitchell and actor and writer Ken Campbell.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Drama series about a 60-year-old private detective. A resident of a retirement home calls on Hetty's help.

programme.title: Ghost Trail
programme.desc: Hamid joins a secret group tracking Syrian regime leaders on the run. His mission takes him to France, pursuing his former torturer for a fateful confrontation. In this manhunt thriller inspired by true events, the past and present collide, exploring themes of justice and redemption. In French with English subtitles.

programme.title: This Is Joan Collins
programme.desc: A feature-length documentary on the life of one of the last surviving actresses from the golden age of Hollywood – Joan Collins. This epic film is told from the ringside as Joan narrates her rollercoaster life story with her inimitable wit and verve. A worldwide television phenomenon with her decade-defining role in Dynasty, Collins shares her extraordinary archive and never before seen home movie footage, giving an intimate glimpse into one of the world’s most iconic figures. Against a backdrop of Collins’s own narration, her story showcases the extraordinary life of a woman who has lived through the glitz, the glamour and the enduring moments of Hollywood history, and survived it all with panache.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about an irrepressible snob. Hyacinth is forced to intervene when Daddy overspends in a toy department, dresses up as a spaceman and proceeds to go on the rampage. Unless the damage is paid for, the owners threaten to apprehend him as a security risk.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: With Frank's wedding impending, Timothy sets his cap at bridesmaid Liz. Will our hero get his head out of the railings in time? Will he forget the ring? And if he gets married, will Mother come on the honeymoon?

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, filmed in 1977 from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Frankie Vaughan, Mike and Bernie Winters, Maryetta Midgley, Zena Millar and members of the Players Theatre, London.

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Dusty Springfield's 1960s variety show, first broadcast on 15 August 1967, with the voices of Madeline Bell, Leslie Duncan and Maggie Stredder. Her special guests are Alf Garnett aka actor Warren Mitchell and actor and writer Ken Campbell.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Drama series about a 60-year-old private detective. A resident of a retirement home calls on Hetty's help.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a programme that follows several thousand pioneering young penguins as they invade Boulders Beach, a busy resort in Cape Town, in an attempt to establish a new colony amid the striped umbrellas and sun loungers.

programme.title: Top Hat
programme.desc: In the quintessential Astaire and Rogers musical, an American dancer staying at a hotel in London falls in love with the guest staying in the room below. However, she gets her wires crossed, convinces herself that the dancer is already hitched and hotfoots it off to Venice. Classic numbers include Cheek to Cheek and Top Hat. This is Fred and Ginger's fourth pairing, but the first with a screenplay written specifically for them.

programme.title: Poems in Their Place: Thomas Hardy
programme.desc: Dr Robert Gittings explores the relationship between poems and the places they were written. He visits Sturminster Newton, where Thomas Hardy enjoyed two very happy years.

programme.title: Michael Palin and the Mystery of Hammershoi
programme.desc: With a passion for art that is rivalled only by travel, Michael Palin combines both in a European journey to discover more about Vilhelm Hammershoi, an enigmatic Danish artist that has fascinated him for years. Curious to see more of Hammershoi's paintings and discover what kind of life the artist lived, Michael searches for clues in London, Holland and Copenhagen.

programme.title: The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: The first real ballet school was founded by King Louis XIV of France in 1669. From Louis's great palace at Versailles, Margot Fonteyn tells the story of Louis's own love of dancing and how it led from the courtly dances of 17th-century France to the worldwide phenomenon of ballet that we know today. At Drottningholm in Sweden, she visits the Court Theatre and sees ballet performed in the original settings and under the original conditions.

programme.title: Dance Rebels: A Story of Modern Dance
programme.desc: Documentary which tells the compelling story of the mavericks like Isadora Duncan, Martha Graham, Merce Cunningham and Pina Bausch, whose radical ideas created modern dance in the 20th century. With historical archive and the first-hand experiences of student dancers from the Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance, it shows the ideas that challenged audiences and changed dance forever. Also, the biggest stars in dance explain what inspired them to create their own groundbreaking choreography. With contributions from Michael Clark, William Forsythe, Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker, Wayne McGregor, Akram Khan, Boris Charmatz and Lea Anderson.

programme.title: Michael Palin and the Mystery of Hammershoi
programme.desc: With a passion for art that is rivalled only by travel, Michael Palin combines both in a European journey to discover more about Vilhelm Hammershoi, an enigmatic Danish artist that has fascinated him for years. Curious to see more of Hammershoi's paintings and discover what kind of life the artist lived, Michael searches for clues in London, Holland and Copenhagen.

programme.title: Nobody's Perfect: The Making of Some Like It Hot
programme.desc: Revealing documentary about the creation and shooting of the acclaimed comedy movie Some Like It Hot. Stars Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis - and other members of the cast and crew, including producer Walter Mirisch - remember the making of writer-director Billy Wilder's classic film. Using extraordinary behind-the-scenes colour footage and candid on-set photographs taken by stills photographer Richard Miller (also interviewed), the documentary provides a surprisingly intimate and revealing but nevertheless hilarious portrait of the making of one of Hollywood's best-loved and greatest films.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: What happens when a literary novelist tries to write popular romantic fiction? To mark 100 years of romance publishers Mills and Boon, literary novelist Stella Duffy takes on the challenge of writing for them. Romantic fiction is a global phenomenon, and Mills and Boon are among the biggest names in the business. The company welcomes submissions from new authors, but as Duffy soon finds out, writing a Mills and Boon is harder than it looks. Help is at hand from the publishers themselves, a prolific Mills and Boon author and some avid romance fans, as Duffy's quest to create the perfect romantic novel takes her from London to Italy on a journey that is both an insight into the art of romantic fiction and the joy and frustration of writing itself.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the 2022/23 competition begins tonight. Two teams of students go head-to-head for a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three maths graduates take on a team featuring a linguistics graduate, an English graduate and an IT developer who is also a proofreader. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random, from 'drip dry' to 'equal by definition' to 'MPs' obligation to vote' to 'exit 300yds ahead'.

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that has been called the British Pompeii due to its state of preservation.

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: In the first episode of a series exploring the history of American art, Andrew Graham-Dixon embarks on an epic journey from east to west, following in the footsteps of the pioneers who built the foundations of modern America. During his journey, he travels to Massachusetts to see the earliest portraits in America depicting the Puritan settlers and visits Pennsylvania to uncover the dark truth behind Benjamin West's most famous painting, the spectacular Treaty of Penn with the Indians. In Philadelphia, he turns the pages of one of the world's most expensive books - John James Audubon's exquisite Birds of America, and explores the wilderness that inspired America's greatest landscape painter, Thomas Cole. He also uncovers the paradox at the heart of America: that progress and innovation have come at a tragic price, the destruction of the unique cultural heritage of Native Americans by European settlers. Andrew's journey takes us to the end of the 19th century and the announcement that the era of westward expansion was officially over.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes explains how America was shaped by Europeans - Spaniards in the south west, Pilgrims in the north east and struggling Cavaliers in Virginia - who saw the New World as both paradise and hell.

programme.title: Leonora Carrington: The Lost Surrealist
programme.desc: British surrealist Leonora Carrington was a key part of the surrealist movement during its heyday in Paris and yet, until recently, remained a virtual unknown in the country of her birth. This film explores her dramatic evolution from British debutante to artist in exile, living out her days in Mexico City, and takes us on a journey into her darkly strange and cinematic world.

programme.title: This Is Joan Collins
programme.desc: A feature-length documentary on the life of one of the last surviving actresses from the golden age of Hollywood – Joan Collins. This epic film is told from the ringside as Joan narrates her rollercoaster life story with her inimitable wit and verve. A worldwide television phenomenon with her decade-defining role in Dynasty, Collins shares her extraordinary archive and never before seen home movie footage, giving an intimate glimpse into one of the world’s most iconic figures. Against a backdrop of Collins’s own narration, her story showcases the extraordinary life of a woman who has lived through the glitz, the glamour and the enduring moments of Hollywood history, and survived it all with panache.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Shiona McCallum visits Fifa HQ in Zurich to see some of the latest tech being used in the World Cup. Martin Sharkey explores how changes in the way we watch content are shaping new technologies. Paul Carter hits the racetrack to see if AI technology systems can make journeys safer, and Andrew Rogers meets the dogs being helped to walk and run again thanks to assistive mobility aids.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Rachel gets into the swing of a cosmopolitan Riviera town, uncovering foodie gems in Nice's charming back streets. She meets some hip young French chefs cooking with bohemian flair at a small local bistro that prides itself on fresh seasonal produce with a cool laid-back vibe. She also visits a socca bar where she learns about the giant chickpea crépe, a speciality of the region. Back in London, Rachel cooks little socca cakes with ratatouille, confit cod with rainbow chard gratin and mini orange trifles with candied carrots.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: The Road to Hope with Fergal Keane
programme.desc: The BBC’s Fergal Keane has spent 40 years witnessing the worst that humanity can do - genocide, war, and famine. So why is he full of hope? The answer lies in the extraordinary bravery he’s encountered in the worst places. In this powerful and humane film by Oscar-winner Alice Doyard, Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present we have real cause for hope.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: Will the Iran war cut Europe’s dependence on energy imports? Soaring energy prices are hurting businesses and household budgets across Europe, so will this crisis change the continent's energy mix for good? Despite efforts to switch to renewables, Europe is still hugely reliant on imported oil and gas. The CEO of Europe’s biggest gas distributor, Italgas, Paolo Gallo explains how new technology can help and Ben Thompson also hears from EU energy commissioner Dan Jørgensen about concerns that moving to solar, wind and nuclear power could just shift Europe’s dependency to other parts of the world.

programme.title: The Smuggling Business: Undercover
programme.desc: Over the past two years, tens of thousands of migrants have crossed the English Channel in small boats from northern France, despite repeated promises by successive governments to stop the trade. This undercover BBC investigation reveals how people smuggling gangs are operating in plain sight inside a migrant camp in Dunkirk. It exposes a highly organised criminal marketplace driven by fierce competition between rival groups, who enforce control over the settlements through fear, violence and intimidation. The film examines efforts by UK authorities to disrupt and dismantle the criminal networks profiting from the crossings, and questions whether those measures are keeping pace with the scale and sophistication of the operation. BBC investigative reporter Josie Hannett also travels to Crowborough, where opposition to a new asylum seeker accommodation site has turned the town into a flashpoint in the national immigration debate. She asks whether current government policy is likely to deter future arrivals, or whether the UK is preparing for a future that will require many more such sites across the south east

programme.title: Discovering the World's Table
programme.desc: In Discovering the World's Table, food writer Nick Kwek embarks on a culinary journey in Poland. He visits two of the country's most vibrant cities Warsaw and Poznan to explore how history and culture inspire the food scene. Starting in Warsaw, Nick meets Olga Bradowska, a local food journalist, to learn about the enduring importance of milk bars in Polish cuisine. They are Polish cafeterias that used to provide low-cost, government-subsidised food during the Communist era. Next, Nick travels to the outskirts of the city to Majlert's farm, a family-run business since the 18th century. He joins renowned chef Marcin Przybysz to gather asparagus for his restaurant, which serves dishes based on recipes from prewar cook books. Poznan has a thriving food scene, from Michelin-starred restaurants to trendy cafes. The city offers a mix of traditional Polish cuisine and modern gastronomic trends. Nick uncovers the secrets behind the world's only legally protected croissant, St Martin's croissant, dating back to 1891. He samples the city's famous potatoes and discovers why a smoked beer is considered 'the champagne of Poland'.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, analyses the possibility of the US forcing regime change in Cuba, examines the effect of the war in Ukraine on Vladimir Putin's standing in Russia and looks at the increasing popularity of prediction markets.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Smuggling Business: Undercover
programme.desc: Over the past two years, tens of thousands of migrants have crossed the English Channel in small boats from northern France, despite repeated promises by successive governments to stop the trade. This undercover BBC investigation reveals how people smuggling gangs are operating in plain sight inside a migrant camp in Dunkirk. It exposes a highly organised criminal marketplace driven by fierce competition between rival groups, who enforce control over the settlements through fear, violence and intimidation. The film examines efforts by UK authorities to disrupt and dismantle the criminal networks profiting from the crossings, and questions whether those measures are keeping pace with the scale and sophistication of the operation. BBC investigative reporter Josie Hannett also travels to Crowborough, where opposition to a new asylum seeker accommodation site has turned the town into a flashpoint in the national immigration debate. She asks whether current government policy is likely to deter future arrivals, or whether the UK is preparing for a future that will require many more such sites across the south east

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Road to Hope with Fergal Keane
programme.desc: The BBC’s Fergal Keane has spent 40 years witnessing the worst that humanity can do - genocide, war, and famine. So why is he full of hope? The answer lies in the extraordinary bravery he’s encountered in the worst places. In this powerful and humane film by Oscar-winner Alice Doyard, Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present we have real cause for hope.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Rachel gets into the swing of a cosmopolitan Riviera town, uncovering foodie gems in Nice's charming back streets. She meets some hip young French chefs cooking with bohemian flair at a small local bistro that prides itself on fresh seasonal produce with a cool laid-back vibe. She also visits a socca bar where she learns about the giant chickpea crépe, a speciality of the region. Back in London, Rachel cooks little socca cakes with ratatouille, confit cod with rainbow chard gratin and mini orange trifles with candied carrots.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ravneet Gill, Luke Holder and The Beefy Boys, and by special guests Stephen Mangan and Sarah Hadland. Drinks expert Carmen O'Neal picks the wines to go with the studio dishes. There are also great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: The 1985 Black Ball Final made snooker legend Dennis Taylor a household name, and there's nowhere that celebrates this giant of sport more than his native Tyrone. Anna visits him at the snooker hall where it all began. Dennis can still remember the soda bread his grandmother used to make over an open fire. Can Anna recreate this childhood recipe? The pair then join fishermen on the largest lake in the UK to celebrate one of Ireland's most unique delicacies - the Lough Neagh eel.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A stunning Staffordshire build reaches its conclusion. Jacqui is in south east London, where an overgrown garden provides a challenge. In Greater Manchester, work has already started at a mid-terrace.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp, experts Ben Cooper and Izzie Balmer and the teams go antique-hunting in Nottinghamshire, before heading to Derby for today’s auction. Natasha meets a former Bargain Hunt contestant with a world record collection of bagpipes, and she has a go herself.

programme.title: Rugby League: Challenge Cup
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors take on Hull Kingston Rovers at Wembley in Rugby League's Challenge Cup.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents this high-stakes quiz, joined by a team of TV stars featuring Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Rev Kate Bottley, Cherry Healey and Babatunde Aleshe as they take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will half their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash for their chosen charities.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Helen George, Ria Lina, Dean McCullough, Josh Widdicombe, Sam Quek and Sam Campbell.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre returns with his Big Show, filmed at London's prestigious Theatre Royal Drury Lane. TV host AJ Odudu plays Send to All, and there's an ambitious indoor skydive prank for the Unexpected Star of the Show. Pop sensation Zara Larsson gives a show-stopping performance, and Michael is back on the road with The Wheel on Wheels to surprise a family somewhere in the UK.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Flynn uncovers more evidence on Bard, but is he too late?

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: It’s Jacob’s birthday, and everyone notices there’s something up with Zoe. She punishes Dan as she focuses on Solomon’s big career break. Dan’s romantic trip might offer hope, but it triggers deeply buried memories for Zoe. Did she choose this island for a reason? Jacob is trying to hide his age from Will, not helped by Will’s Gen Z mates gatecrashing their night out at a local taverna. When Jess goes on the attack to save her own skin, Zoe is left with nowhere to go but out.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Highlights of the 2026 UEFA Champions League final between PSG and Arsenal in Budapest. Twenty years on from their last appearance in the European Cup final, Arsenal are seeking to win their first Champions League title. However, they face a formidable test in the reigning European champions, Paris Saint-Germain. Jason Mohammad presents, with expert analysis from Nedum Onuoha and Matt Upson. programme.desc: The big names behind the big stories. Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: The Book of Life
programme.desc: A museum tour guide explains the Mexican tradition of the Book of Life to a group of bored schoolchildren. They become entranced by the story of Manolo and Joaquin, who vie for the love of the beautiful and feisty Maria.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin wants to run for the hills because of a tricky legal issue in Devon. Jacqui is in Eastbourne, where work is already under way at an end-of-terrace. And Dion goes to Northampton, where a smelly cellar is hard to cope with!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles is in Peterborough for one of the largest antiques fairs in the UK. He’s joined by Bargain Hunt regular Colin Young, who has some top tips for porcelain collectors. Experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell guide the red and blue teams around the fair, but who will go home empty-handed and who is making a big score?

programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is in south London meeting Christians who are volunteering their time and talents to help others.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television programmes. Whether you’ve seen something you’ve loved or loathed on the BBC, get in touch with your point of view. Email: pov@bbc.co.uk X: @bbcPoV Facebook: Search for BBC Points of View

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A pair of artists look to Alistair Appleton to help them find an inspiring rural retreat in Somerset where they can further indulge their creativity. Alistair also meets a young couple who moved to the countryside in search of the good life and never looked back, despite ups and downs along the way.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Amazon, where extraordinary animal behaviours reveal the rainforest’s hidden stories. In southern Brazil, over a million turtle hatchlings make a perilous dash to the river as black caiman prey on them – a newly filmed behaviour. In Venezuela, a young harpy eagle that has been watched from its earliest days endures intense storms while awaiting its father’s return with food, providing new understanding of life high in the canopy. Also in Brazil, giant river otters hunt together in a coordinated effort, displaying unique social bonds as they corral fish in the shallows. Army ants in Ecuador break down their bivouac and march through the forest with a level of precision and cooperation seldom seen. In the Pantanal, caiman engage in an elaborate courtship display, their movements sending ripples across the water – a striking look at an ancient ritual.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The frozen north, where survival means constantly adapting to an unforgiving landscape. In Hudson Bay, a rare encounter between the region's top predators unfolds as wolves and polar bears clash over a single carcass – a behaviour never caught on camera until now. Under the glow of the northern lights, snowshoe hares use their large feet to escape lynx in the vast boreal forest. In Alberta, sharp-tailed grouse put on a show like never before, practising their courtship dances under car headlights. Off Alaska’s coast, male walruses engage in curious vocal displays, singing together under the sunset. In summer, millions of salmon begin their epic journey upstream, while newborn caribou navigate dangerous river crossings on their first migratory trek. Life here is a series of bold moves and dramatic encounters, in a place where the stakes are always high.

programme.title: Shrek 2
programme.desc: Animated sequel following the grumpy ogre and his bride as they head for the land of Far Far Away to meet her parents. As Shrek contends with his new in-laws, the wicked Fairy Godmother sets in motion a dastardly scheme to scupper his marriage to Fiona. And will any of them survive at the hands of dandy assassin Puss in Boots? Featuring the voices of Mike Myers (Shrek), Eddie Murphy (Donkey), Cameron Diaz (Princess Fiona), Antonio Banderas (Puss in Boots), Julie Andrews (Queen), John Cleese (King), Rupert Everett (Prince Charming), Jennifer Saunders (Fairy Godmother), Aron Warner (Wolf), Kelly Asbury (Page/Elf/Nobleman/Nobleman's Son), Cody Cameron (Pinocchio/Three Pigs) and Conrad Vernon (Gingerbread Man/Cedric/Announcer/Muffin Man).

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Adam Henson are in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, for the Balmoral Show, the region’s largest agricultural and food event, welcoming more than 100,000 visitors each year. As the show celebrates its 157th year, Sean and Adam meet the people who make it such a vital gathering for the region’s farming community and explore how events like this are crucial to their future. Adam explores heritage crafts that community members are determined to preserve, and he joins families showing their prize livestock. Sean takes charge of the stopwatch at the tractor-handling competition and gets hands-on with traditional local produce that is valuable to the region’s economy.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A special episode commemorating the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings in June 1944, filmed in Normandy and Portsmouth, and featuring testimony from those who were there.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: When a runner overdoses in an actor’s trailer, Janie and John are called to investigate. On the set of an epic Welsh TV drama, the team interview the crew to solve the crime.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the devastating defeat at Wembley, Southgate’s guilt weighs heavily on his conscience. When the fans then turn on Southgate, accusing him of prioritising politics over performance, he begins to doubt himself ahead of a controversial World Cup in Qatar.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Nice Guys
programme.desc: In 1970s Los Angeles, a mismatched pair of private eyes investigate a missing girl and the mysterious death of a porn star. Comedy starring Ryan Gosling and Russell Crowe.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police concerns grow about the mental state of two missing teens - 16-year-old Larissa has disappeared after an argument with her mum, and 18-year-old Bobby may be a suicide risk.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright hears from Jane, who awoke in the middle of the night to discover two masked men breaking into her elderly parents' house. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan gives some top tips on what to do if you come home and find out you’ve been burgled. Matt also hears from Tami, whose doorbell captured the moment she took on a gang of wannabe bike thieves.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two houses in Worksop and Wakefield are bought at auction by developers who plan to rent them out. Meanwhile in Dewsbury, a midwife and her builder husband buy a house that has not been lived in for a decade, but they have big plans to create a forever home for them and their four children.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A hundred years ago in Yorkshire, it wasn’t ketchup that people added to their food - it was a delicious locally made relish. By the turn of the 20th century, 30 million bottles of Yorkshire Relish were being made in Leeds each year and shipped around the world. Made of vinegar, sugar, fruits and 27 spices, it was the go-to sauce for generations, right up until the 1950s. In the city where it was made, Danny Sebastian savours a collection of advertising items that celebrate this once-popular condiment. If that doesn’t whet your appetite, enjoy two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the antiques stalls at Wetherby Racecourse. On the red team are twins and classic car fans Mike and Joe, who hope to breakdance their way to victory - as long as they don’t break the bank first! Against them are mum and daughter Sue and Sarah, who plan on using their cha-cha-charm to get a good price. But which team will be dancing all the way to a profit at Caroline Hawley’s auction?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie discovers the power of caring volunteers, and how mind and body work together to help people recover and move forward.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by his footballing hero, former England and Newcastle United captain Alan Shearer. The football superstar and Match of the Day pundit takes time out to go mackerel fishing off the Northumberland coast, where the pair spot a pod of dolphins. Robson takes Alan to Eshott Airfield near Morpeth to take to the skies in a 1940s Tiger Moth.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After 30 years in what was meant to be a temporary home in the city of Stoke-on-Trent, today’s couple is finally ready to make their escape to Wiltshire to be closer to family. With a firm belief that separate bathrooms are the secret to a happy marriage, they turn to Ginny Buckley to find them their perfect property. Ginny takes a break from the house search to meet someone already living the Wiltshire country dream by creating jams, chutneys and pickles.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris and Frances are in Leicester to help Hector and Claudia transform their blank new-build plot into a Cypriot cooking garden for a growing family. Uneven grass, a sloped area behind the garage and a bare brick wall have left the space difficult to use. The couple dream of a garden reflecting Hector’s Cypriot heritage and their shared love of food, with a dedicated outdoor cooking and dining area, alongside a vegetable patch for their first child, Arthur, to learn about growing food. They also want to retain a grassy area for their dog, Lilo, to play in. Claudia and Hector met at Bristol University eight years ago, and after marrying in 2023, they celebrated their honeymoon in Rhodes with a romantic cooking class in the mountains. Now they’re ready to bring their Mediterranean-inspired vision home. Can the Garden Rescue team turn this blank canvas into a flourishing Cypriot cooking garden on a £4,000 budget?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, friends Kenny, Nicola, Lyndsey and Craig – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Yolande helps Denise as she processes life-changing news, and Ian is blissfully unaware that his happy life is about to change.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Giving birth in the UK is getting more dangerous; the number of women dying in childbirth is up 20% in the last ten years. Now, in Nottingham, the largest-ever maternity inquiry is underway, reviewing the medical records of thousands of births for signs of avoidable harm. Reporting for Panorama, Michael Buchanan hears powerful testimonies: from mothers who weren’t listened to, staff who blew the whistle but were ignored, and from parents whose babies’ tragic deaths were dismissed as ‘just bad luck’. The programme also hears from the new chief executive of the Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust, who has promised change. As families unite to demand answers, Panorama investigates how systemic neglect, inadequate staffing and a reluctance to learn from mistakes have led to heartbreak.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The team take on a group of scammers pretending to be from the UK’s National Crime Agency and frightening their victims into believing they will be arrested if they do not prove their identity. They have locked on to a student in Preston who thinks her National Insurance number has been compromised. The scammers say the only way to fix things is to buy gift cards and hand over the numbers on them. As the hub team try to reach her by phone, the ground team rush to the nearby supermarket to stop her buying the gift cards in person. Can the teams intercept the scammers and their victim in time?

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: As Euro 2024 begins, England arrive as favourites for Southgate’s final tournament. Gareth is forced to make some challenging changes to the team and puts his trust in a new generation. When the team struggles unexpectedly and pressure mounts, Gareth wonders how his story will end.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Guest host Katherine Ryan, journalist Jo Coburn and comedian John Tothill join Ian and Paul as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Football’s New Generation
programme.desc: The intimate story of England's newest recruits, charting their rise from grassroots heroes to Europe's biggest stage. Featuring Cole Palmer, Kobbie Mainoo, Ollie Watkins and Marc Guehi, the documentary reveals their personal stories for the first time, with contributions from their loved ones, family and friends, as well as the veterans who mentored them to the world stage. The film charts their paths from humble beginnings, revealing for the first time the personal battles that culminated in a new breed of players representing England at Euro 2024.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Zoe Ball follows in her ancestors’ footsteps in a journey that takes her from Scotland to Cornwall as she uncovers stories of resilience and the importance of family over place.

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Sian is frustrated that she still hasn’t been able to contact the Gower family who lived at the farmhouse – David, a former headmaster, his wife Rose-Mary and their son John-Paul. But there is a breakthrough of another kind when the current owner of Penyffordd Farm gets in touch and invites her over to see the house she’s heard so much about. Sian is welcomed in by Michael, who bought the house in Flintshire from the Gowers despite stories that it was haunted. He never believed their accounts, but he has heard rumours of the gravestone of a teenage girl called Jane Jones, which he suspects might still be on the property somewhere. The archive from Dr Daniels suggests that moving this gravestone was believed to be the precursor for all the strange happenings in the house, so it's the next lead for Sian to chase. Ghost or not, is there any historical evidence to suggest there really is a girl buried at Penyffordd Farm – and if so, why would she be buried in the garden? Sian tracks down a genealogist to see if there’s any truth to the story and finally gets an opportunity to speak to a member of the Gower family…

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Sian Eleri’s investigation into the haunting of Penyffordd Farm continues as multiple witnesses claim to have seen a hooded figure. Could they all have imagined the same thing?

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Sian's investigation into an allegedly haunted house in north Wales concludes. Can she track down the last family members and solve whether this was paranormal or all a hoax?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Sports mockumentary about GB Paralympian Darren Silverstone, who stole the hearts of a nation before his infamous chokey-o in Tokyo. For the first time, he tells his story - and starts his glorious comeback. In Tokyo 2020, Darren Silverstone was the Paras GB poster boy. (He was on yoghurts, too.) He cruised to the final and charmed the nation along the way with his signature dance move, the dazzle. But as he took that final bend ready to take gold, Silverstone lost his head and celebrated too soon - and in the process, became a national figure of fun. His subsequent journey in the public eye brought approval and consternation in equal measure: from those unforgettably self-deprecating betting adverts to his turn on The Masked Singer as Breadbin. Darren’s best friend and pilot rider in Tokyo, little Paul Potter, was never seen again in the public eye. The two haven’t spoken since. And now, Silverstone’s back. Ready to take to the tandem at Paris 2024. But is he ready? Who will he be riding with? This is I Am Darren.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul is lurking in the shadows, attempting to fix the competition…

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie’s been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: In the near future, the Doctor and Clara find themselves on a space shuttle making a suicide mission to the moon. Crash-landing on the lunar surface, they find a mining base full of corpses, vicious spider-like creatures poised to attack, and a terrible dilemma. When Clara turns to the Doctor for help, she gets the shock of her life.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara are on the most beautiful train in history, speeding among the stars of the future. But a deadly creature is stalking the passengers. Once you see the horrifying mummy, you only have 66 seconds to live. No exceptions, no reprieve. As the Doctor races against the clock, Clara sees him at his deadliest and most ruthless. Will he work out how to defeat the mummy? Start the clock!

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Tilly's fiancee Rupert is on army leave and their wedding plans are in full swing. Miranda is set up with Rupert's ghastly best man Ping Pong Charlie at the same time as Stevie and Clive set her up with a 'special someone' else. Her date with the 'special someone' gives Miranda a new-found confidence that Ping Pong Charlie and Rupert find irresistible. How will she shake off Ping Pong Charlie? Should she tell Tilly about Rupert's advances, and more importantly, how long will the 'special someone' wait for a kiss?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda and Stevie compete to be best friends with the new waitress at the restaurant, Tamara. The trouble is that Tamara is 22, and they both find keeping up with an energetic 20-something surprisingly tiring. Their age becomes an issue after an art class, followed by all-night karaoke, followed by a swimming experience that puts Miranda face-to-face with her nudity issues. With Miranda and Stevie united, will they keep up with Tamara, or will they just bung their slippers on, get a hot chocolate and settle down in front of Midsomer Murders?

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There's a storm blowing in Finglas, and it soon claims its first victim when the pipes burst in Winnie and Sharon's house. Thank goodness for Mrs Brown, who is on hand to offer a roof over their heads while repairs are carried out. It's not long before there's a storm brewing inside the Brown household too. Agnes and Cathy discover that living with best friends isn't the picnic they'd imagined. Agnes has yet more on her plate when she receives news of the death of a neighbour she never really knew - or even liked - and is asked by Father Damian to do her eulogy. And there is Bono's thirteenth birthday to host. It might all seem too much for Agnes, and then Buster drops a bombshell.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There’s bad news for Winnie when Agnes discovers Peggy Piper the Pernod poisoner is out on appeal and could be coming back to Finglas. This could mean Winnie has to give evidence against her again as she was the one who saw Peggy poison her husband. Question is, is Peggy guilty or innocent? There's only one way of knowing for sure - and that's by meeting Peggy herself. Meanwhile, Buster loses the expensive tank costume Dermot’s hired for their latest promotion and must find it before it's too late. Sue Vincent guest stars.

programme.title: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret.
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Margaret is uprooted from her life in New York City for the suburbs of New Jersey, going through the messy and tumultuous throes of puberty with new friends in a new school. She relies on her mother, Barbara, who is also struggling to adjust to life outside the big city, and her adoring grandmother, Sylvia, who isn’t happy they moved away and likes to remind them every chance she gets. Based on Judy Blume’s classic novel.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul is lurking in the shadows, attempting to fix the competition…

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie’s been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Now that Gaz and Donna are divorced, Gaz decides the time has come to propose to Janet. Scared that she is losing all her friends, in particular Gaz, Donna insists on helping him organise the proposal, much to the disapproval of Tim who worries she's holding onto the past and not moving on. Gaz and Donna enjoy spending time together, possibly too much, and it conjures up old feelings. Wesley gives Janet a job on the market stall and teaches her the trade, but she struggles as she suffers increasingly from a stomach ache. When the pain gets so bad and she is rushed to hospital, she asks Wesley to phone Gaz, but he is nowhere to be found.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet arrives back from hospital with Wesley and Tim, minus an appendix, and there is no sign of Gaz. Janet catches him out when he comes creeping back into the house later on. When he finds out that she has spent the night with Wesley holding her hand he blurts out that he has slept with Donna, so Janet throws him out. Louise tells Tim she is desperate for a boyfriend, just as ex-boyband member Scott enters the pub. He tries to woo her, but she is convinced there must be something wrong with him. There is, but it's not what she thinks. Gaz is torn between the two girls and although he decides to stay with Janet for the sake of Corinthian, when he goes to tell Donna he realises he loves her too and can't be without her. Janet and Wesley go to the flat only to discover them in bed together once more. They demand he makes a choice between Janet and Donna.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A social media meltdown forces Geoffrey to reassess his life and values.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: In the near future, the Doctor and Clara find themselves on a space shuttle making a suicide mission to the moon. Crash-landing on the lunar surface, they find a mining base full of corpses, vicious spider-like creatures poised to attack, and a terrible dilemma. When Clara turns to the Doctor for help, she gets the shock of her life.

programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara are on the most beautiful train in history, speeding among the stars of the future. But a deadly creature is stalking the passengers. Once you see the horrifying mummy, you only have 66 seconds to live. No exceptions, no reprieve. As the Doctor races against the clock, Clara sees him at his deadliest and most ruthless. Will he work out how to defeat the mummy? Start the clock!

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max confronts the Mitchells about his suspicions, Gina gives Eddie the cold shoulder, and Zack gets some unwelcome news.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, and Eddie and Harry clash.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It’s the day of Vicki and Ross’s wedding, but will it go ahead? Priya gets a shock.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at Vicki and Ross’s wedding, and Denise receives some news.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: After solving the Andie Bell case, Pip is forced to confront the consequences of what her investigation exposed. It may have cleared Sal’s name, but it tore Cara’s family apart, and the guilt weighs heavily on Pip. In an attempt to rebuild her relationship with Cara, Pip releases a podcast to tell the world the real story. The only person yet to face justice is Max Hastings, and as his trial approaches, Pip is determined to help his victims find closure. However, the prosecution’s case begins to falter when Becca Bell withdraws from testifying. Unwilling to accept this, Pip musters the courage to visit Becca in prison with the hope of convincing her that giving evidence still matters. Pip wants to focus on supporting Ravi at the upcoming memorial for his brother, but when a mysterious note promising information on Max appears in her locker, Pip must make a difficult decision. Little Kilton comes together to say goodbye to both Andie Bell and Sal Singh. The end of one dark chapter gives way to another, as Cara finds herself in a dangerous situation, and Jamie Reynolds, Connor’s brother and a key witness in Max’s trial, disappears without warning.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie’s recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Arthur the King
programme.desc: Over the course of ten days and 435 miles, an unbreakable bond is forged between pro adventure racer Michael Light and a scrappy street dog companion dubbed Arthur. Desperate for one last chance to win, Michael convinces a sponsor to back him and a team of athletes for the Adventure Racing World Championship in the Dominican Republic. As the team is pushed to their outer limits of endurance in the race, Arthur redefines what victory, loyalty and friendship truly mean. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britain’s most maligned ethnic minorities.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Spending time with people who regularly steal from shops, Stacey gets to grips with the shoplifting epidemic and what it’s like for the frontline staff trying to prevent thefts.

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: A darkly comic drama about three stubbornly optimistic siblings with a terrible secret. When their mum seemingly goes missing, Tiana, Tionne and Tanika will do anything to keep it quiet so that they can stay together as a family. But feistily resilient and fiercely loyal as they are, can they really outwit the authorities and carry on life under the radar? Tiana, the eldest, takes charge, determined to hold the household together until she turns 18 and can legally keep her siblings under one roof. But keeping their lives on track without a parent is a battle she is barely equipped for - especially as the lies pile up, the bills go unpaid and the world around them starts closing in. Things get complicated when Tiana is forced to allow her mum’s hapless drug dealer - Dr Feelgood – to move in with them after he realises that they are home alone. It’s an unholy union. Feelgood might be able to bring money and even a sense of security into their little flat, but he is unpredictable and chaotic, and Tionne holds him responsible for their mother’s disappearance. Furious at Tiana for letting the 'enemy' into their home, Tionne becomes hellbent on getting rid of Feelgood for ever.

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings’ secret’s exposed. Tiana plans their escape but fails and is forced to confront their estranged dad. Feelgood offers to help, but there's a catch - he wants to move in.

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A desolate train track seems the perfect spot to end it all – until someone else turns up with the same idea. Awkward.
